title: SHC Configuration Replication Lag (_internal)
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=*SHC* OR component=*shcluster*
| search ("replication" OR "bundle" OR "lag") AND (log_level=WARN OR log_level=ERROR)
| stats count by host, message
| sort -count
description: Lagging conf replication causes inconsistent searches and failed lookups across members.
value: Lagging conf replication causes inconsistent searches and failed lookups across members.
implementation: Prefer REST `| rest /services/shcluster/member/members` for `last_conf_replication` where available. Alert on WARN/ERROR about replication lag or failed bundles.
visualization: Table (members with replication issues), Single value (max lag seconds).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` (SHC, replication, `apply_bundle`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Prefer REST `| rest /services/shcluster/member/members` for `last_conf_replication` where available. Alert on WARN/ERROR about replication lag or failed bundles.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=*SHC* OR component=*shcluster*
| search ("replication" OR "bundle" OR "lag") AND (log_level=WARN OR log_level=ERROR)
| stats count by host, message
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SHC Configuration Replication Lag (_internal)** ‚Äî Lagging conf replication causes inconsistent searches and failed lookups across members.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` (SHC, replication, `apply_bundle`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (members with replication issues), Single value (max lag seconds).
grandmaExplanation: Lagging conf replication causes inconsistent searches and failed lookups across members ‚Äî so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
